headline: Verizon Deepens Disaster Readiness With Digital Twin And Satellite Expansion
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) has rolled out advanced Digital Twin technology using AI and drones to inspect and model its network for disaster readiness. The company has expanded its multi orbit satellite fleet to support critical communications when ground infrastructure is disrupted. Verizon launched the V Team Disaster Response Corps in partnership with the American Red Cross to coordinate support in emergencies. Verizon Communications, trading at $47.34, is adding a new layer of...

headline: Lumen Posts Wider-Than-Expected Q1 Loss, Revenues Drop Y/Y
summary: LUMN posts wider Q1 loss as revenues fall 9%, while strategic sales hit 51% and an Alkira deal targets faster-growing east-west cloud connectivity.

headline: US device promotions: the subsidy wrapper hiding the real price—service revenue
summary: In April, telcos, OEMs, and retailers rolled out 960 smartphone promos, down 28% month-over-month with trade-ins, bundles, and new line offers leading the promotional activity.

raw.headline: US device promotions: the subsidy wrapper hiding the real price—service revenue

headline: 33-year-old brings in $192,000 a month negotiating car deals for strangers—how he built a fully remote business
summary: With car prices soaring, Tomi built a business negotiating car deals for clients. He now has 15 remote employees and brings in $2.6 million a year. He shares exactly how to turn a practical skill into a million-dollar business or side hustle.

headline: Verizon Unveils Digital Twin Technology and Expanded Satellite Fleet in Preparation for the 2026 Hurricane Season
summary: From AI-powered virtual modeling to a fleet of 2,600 satellite assets including multi-orbit satellite equipped off-road vehicles, Verizon demonstrates the latest in its network resiliency ecosystemKey Takeaways Verizon's new Digital Twin technology uses drone-captured 3D imagery and AI to instantly identify the exact location of network damage after a storm, allowing engineers to prioritize repairs, speed up restoration, and enhance safety by reducing the need for on-site visual inspections.To e

headline: Verizon CEO doubles down on removing free offers for customers
summary: Verizon has been working to recover from several years of elevated customer losses in its wireless business.
